agent_reply: Yep, that log strongly suggests the SCO audio link is getting stuck in `CONNECTING`, and our retry task was making overlapping `esp_hf_client_connect_audio()` calls while the previous one had not resolved yet. That is probably not helping the phone keep the route.

I patched [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp) so it now tracks `g_hfp_audio_connecting` separately from `g_hfp_audio_connected`. `connect_hfp_audio_once()` will now explicitly skip attempts when audio is already `CONNECTING` or connected.
So the next run should look less noisy:
- no repeated retry calls unless the stack reports `AUDIO_STATE_DISCONNECTED`
If it still disconnects when the call starts, the next suspect is not retry behavior anymore; it will be SCO negotiation or role/profile mismatch.

agent_reply: The big one: `hfp_outgoing_audio()` now only calls the userspace mSBC encoder after the callback payload has been positively detected as encoded mSBC, not while it is still `Unknown`: [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1039>). That prevents the speculative encoder call that led into `EncPacking`.

I also fixed the deeper ticking time bomb: the bundled Bluedroid mSBC wrapper was getting linked against ESP-IDFŌĆÖs `SBC_Encoder` symbol. user_prompt: can you tell me, if a not-user-initiated disconnection happens, what does the bluetooth stack do? does it just sit disconnected? does it attempt reconnection?
agent_reply: In our code, a non-user-initiated HFP disconnect does **not** actively reconnect by itself.
- If `g_disconnect_requested == false`, the disconnect event moves `BtManager` to `State::Reconnecting`.
- It clears the active connection/audio flags and tells `CallManager` Bluetooth disconnected.
- The radio remains **connectable but non-discoverable**, so the phone can reconnect to it.
- A UI/action can also call `BtManager::connectToMac(...)` again, because that function allows calls while state is `Idle` or `Reconnecting`.

It does **not** call `BtManager::disconnect()`, `shutdown()`, or change Bluetooth scan/connectability.

Important catch: Bluetooth is currently initialized as **non-connectable / non-discoverable** in `BluetoothManager.cpp`, and `setConnectableNonDiscoverable()` exists but does not appear to be used in normal startup. So host-initiated ŌĆ£incomingŌĆØ connections may already be disabled most of the time, but not because memo recording started.
This is **not already implemented**. In [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:244), `handle_pending_bluetooth_recording()` currently does this:
So if Bluetooth connects while memo recording is already active, the pending Bluetooth recording event is ignored. The view stays in memo mode.
1. Decide where Bluetooth should be connectable.
If you want incoming connections during memo, call `BtManager::setConnectableNonDiscoverable()` during normal idle/memo-capable operation, and avoid it during Wi-Fi/AP modes.
2. Add a ŌĆ£promote memo to Bluetooth UIŌĆØ path.
Do not call `AudioFileRecorder::startRecording()` again. Just switch the existing `RecordingView` with `configureBluetoothMode()` and force redraw.
3. Unchoke mic-to-Bluetooth without restarting the file.

Memo mode currently chokes `micToBluetoothFifo()`. Expose a helper in `RecordingViewCallbacks` that calls the existing restore logic, keeps `AudioManager` in mic mode, and does not touch the recorder.
If recording is already active, promote the view and audio routing instead of starting a new recording.
5. Optional but smart: clear `AudioManager::bluetoothToSpeakerFifo()` when the user first switches to speaker, so old buffered call audio does not play late.

The file write should be fine: `AudioFileRecorder` already records both Bluetooth-to-file and mic-to-file FIFOs into the same open file. agent_reply: Yep, the culprit is pretty clear now.
| good custom sdkconfig | `0x1dd73` / 122,227 B | fits, about 7.8 KB headroom |
| bad default config | `0x1fd2f` / 130,351 B | overflows by 308 B |
| bad custom framework zips | `0x1ff43` / 130,883 B | overflows by 840 B |

The big IRAM delta is not mainly Bluetooth. It is `libc.a` being pulled into IRAM. In the bad/custom-package map, about 8.9 KB extra comes from routines like `mktime`, `tzset_r`, `findfp`, `fvwrite`, `fflush`, `strtol`, etc. That matches the `CONFIG_SPIRAM_CACHE_LIB*IN_IRAM` settings being effectively ŌĆ£onŌĆØ in the linker script.

The custom package fixes the missing HFP symbol: `esp_hf_client_outgoing_data_ready` is present in the custom-package map, while the default build only has the unresolved reference from `BluetoothManager.cpp`.
The remaining failure is this file:
It is still default/upstream and explicitly places lots of `libc.a:*` objects into `.iram0.text`. The good buildŌĆÖs generated linker script does not:
For the custom zip approach, replace these files in both package trees, then publish as a new release tag like `r2`:
2. Copy good sdkconfig header for consistency:
- to both `esp32/dio_qspi/include/sdkconfig.h` and `esp32/qio_qspi/include/sdkconfig.h` in the libs package
- also update the nested copy under `custom-framework-arduinoespressif32/tools/esp32-arduino-libs/...`
3. Do not package `.piopm` files if you can avoid it. Let PlatformIO regenerate them.
4. Use a new URL/tag in `platformio.ini`; do not overwrite the same `r1` asset and expect PlatformIO to notice reliably.

Also, current [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:8) has the custom package URLs but the `custom_sdkconfig` block was removed. That is why the build no longer regenerates the good `sections.ld`. Either restore the `custom_sdkconfig` block as a safety net, or make the custom package fully self-contained by fixing `sections.ld` as above. My preference: do both until this is boringly stable.

user_prompt: the crash was upon completion of pairing
agent_reply: Yep, that explains the stack traces. Pairing completion was letting the phone immediately kick off HFP service setup, which is the fragile Bluedroid path.
I changed it so boot/pairing use Bluetooth GAP only, and HFP is initialized lazily when you actually connect to a saved host:
- Changed `setup()` in [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp) to use GAP-only init, while still storing audio callbacks.
- Changed `startPairing()` in [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp) to avoid HFP during pairing, and deinit HFP before pairing if it was already up.
- Left the internal HFP debug flags disabled in [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini), since theyŌĆÖre still too close to the WDT path for comfort.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, build passes.

agent_reply: That trace is still the same Bluedroid HFP SLC path, now specifically dying while sending `AT+BAC` for codec negotiation.

I added a targeted workaround: [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp) now wraps BluedroidŌĆÖs internal `bta_hf_client_send_at_bac()` and skips straight to the next SLC step (`AT+CIND`). [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) adds the linker wrap flag. This should avoid the crashing `AT+BAC` path and force the connection toward default/CVSD audio instead of mSBC/wideband.
I also kept the previous GAP-only pairing changes, so pairing no longer starts HFP immediately.

Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, build passes. The tradeoff is that HFP audio may be narrowband only for now, but that is a much better place to debug from than ŌĆ£phone click detonates inside Bluedroid.ŌĆØ

agent_reply: I tightened the Bluetooth connection path around that crash.

The key changes are in [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:17): HFP now starts lazily, waits for the async HFP profile-ready event before calling `esp_hf_client_connect()`, resets those readiness flags on failure/deinit, and skips the unstable Bluedroid `AT+BAC` codec negotiation path. I also made pairing use Bluetooth/GAP only, then [main_callbacks.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main_callbacks.cpp:530) reboots after the pairing confirmation is dismissed so the later phone connection starts from a clean BT stack.

Build passes. Next test path: pair phone, dismiss confirmation, let The Fly reboot, then click the paired phone item and try connecting again. A remaining crash after that clean reboot would strongly point into the prebuilt ESP32 BT stack config, likely WBS/HFP codec support or verbose BT trace settings, rather than the app-side connection flow.

agent_reply: YouŌĆÖre right: this is the normal HFP connect path, not pairing/NVS.
`__wrap_bta_hf_client_send_at_bac` -> `bta_hf_client_send_at_cind` -> RFCOMM write -> FreeRTOS queue corruption-ish crash.

So my linker wrapper was the wrong layer. I removed it from [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp) and removed the `--wrap=bta_hf_client_send_at_bac` flag from [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini). Build still passes with:
What IŌĆÖd do for the next custom package rebuild:

But that alone may not be enough, because EspressifŌĆÖs HFP client source still advertises codec negotiation through `BTC_HF_CLIENT_FEATURES`, and `bta_hf_client_slc_seq()` sends `AT+BAC` if the phone advertises codec support. For a narrow ŌĆ£make normal phone HFP connect stable againŌĆØ package, IŌĆÖd also remove `BTA_HF_CLIENT_FEAT_CODEC` from `BTC_HF_CLIENT_FEATURES`, or patch `bta_hf_client_slc_seq()` so it only sends `AT+BAC` when both peer and local features include codec negotiation.

The current package already has `CONFIG_BT_BTC_TASK_STACK_SIZE=8192` and `CONFIG_BT_BTU_TASK_STACK_SIZE=8192`, so this does not look like the old obvious ŌĆ£BT task stack too smallŌĆØ problem. The codec negotiation path is the hot spot.

agent_reply: Implemented and verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`.
- Bluetooth now starts full HFP at boot again, so incoming host connections can be accepted normally.
- Pairing no longer deinitializes HFP or makes the radio non-connectable.
- Pairing mode suppresses auto-recording immediately, so a phone can connect after pairing without starting a recording.
- Pairing success dialog now falls back to `FLYGUI_VIEW_MAIN` before reboot, so it cannot land on splash if reboot is delayed or interrupted.
- Bluetooth long-hold delete now shows the trash icon as soon as the hold threshold is reached.
- Unpair no longer immediately saves through the path that re-reads bond state too early, which should fix the ŌĆ£delete twice before it disappearsŌĆØ behavior.
Build passes. One note: `pio` regenerated `src/version.c` during the build.
